prompt: As of June 2026, what is the state of the art in LLM REQUEST ROUTING (deciding per-request which model to call to optimize cost and quality)? Cover and rank: RouteLLM, vLLM Semantic Router, NotDiamond, Martian, multi-signal routers, and cascade/escalation approaches. Give how they measure and trade off cost vs quality, what signals they use (embedding, classifier, predicted-quality), and any 2025-2026 work. Cite papers and repos with dates. Then: for a gateway that currently routes by intent/namespace to a primary+fallback and wants to become 'cheapest model that still meets a measured quality bar', what signals and labels must it persist, and what is the simplest router design that beats static routing in 2026?
content:
**As of June 2026, LLM request routing (per-query model selection for cost-quality optimization) has matured from simple rule-based or intent/namespace routing to learned, signal-driven, and cascaded systems.** Open-source frameworks (RouteLLM, vLLM Semantic Router) and commercial offerings (NotDiamond, Martian) dominate, alongside hybrid multi-signal and cascade/escalation patterns. Progress in 2025–2026 emphasizes multi-signal fusion, RL for sequential decisions, unified routing+cascading frameworks, and production-grade safety/caching integration.[[1]](https://vllm.ai/blog/2026-01-05-vllm-sr-iris)[[2]](https://openreview.net/forum?id=AAl89VNNy1)

### RouteLLM (lm-sys)
- **Overview and dates**: Framework + routers from LM-Sys (GitHub lm-sys/RouteLLM, blog July 2024; arXiv 2406.18665 June 2024; ICLR 2025 poster). Trains lightweight routers on human preference data (with augmentation) to route between strong (e.g., GPT-4 Turbo) and weak (e.g., Llama-2-70B or Mixtral) models. Achieves ~40%+ cost savings at equivalent quality vs. static or commercial baselines on MT-Bench; open routers/datasets on HF.[[3]](https://github.com/lm-sys/routellm)[[4]](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.18665)[[5]](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-07-01-routellm/)
- **Cost-quality tradeoff**: Explicitly optimizes the frontier—routes to weaker/cheaper model when preference data indicates it suffices. Evaluates via win rates or quality parity at lower blended cost.
- **Signals**: Preference-trained classifiers/routers (often embedding- or feature-based models predicting routing decision directly). Not purely semantic; leverages pairwise preferences.
- **2025–2026 work**: Core 2024 release; used as baseline in later papers (e.g., comparisons in vLLM and cascade works).

### vLLM Semantic Router
- **Overview and dates**: vllm-project/semantic-router (initial Sept 2025; v0.1 “Iris” Jan 2026 with major plugin-chain architecture; arXiv 2603.04444 ~2026). Signal-driven MoM (Mixture-of-Models) router integrated with vLLM/Envoy. Handles classification, safety (jailbreak/PII), semantic caching, hallucination detection, and intent/complexity routing across modalities.[[1]](https://vllm.ai/blog/2026-01-05-vllm-sr-iris)[[6]](https://github.com/vllm-project/semantic-router)
- **Cost-quality tradeoff**: Semantic classification routes simple/light queries to small/fast models and complex/reasoning ones to heavy models; multi-signal decisions enable fine-grained policies (cost, privacy, quality). Significant latency/token savings reported (e.g., selective reasoning).
- **Signals**: Multi-signal extraction (BERT/ModernBERT classifiers for intent/complexity/reasoning needs; keyword heuristics; embeddings/similarity; safety signals). Composable via Boolean rules (AND/OR) into plugin chains for decisions. Extends beyond single classifiers to heterogeneous signals.[[7]](https://vllm.ai/blog/2025-11-19-signal-decision)[[8]](https://arxiv.org/html/2603.04444v4)
- **2025–2026 work**: Rapid evolution to signal-decision architecture (Nov 2025+); strong community/production focus; comparisons position it as advanced multi-signal system.

### NotDiamond
- **Overview and dates**: Commercial router (launched ~July 2024; Python SDK active into 2025; RoRF random-forest framework). Trains custom “meta-models” on user evaluation data to pick the best LLM per query; also offers pre-trained routers (e.g., chat/code). Curates awesome-ai-model-routing list.[[9]](https://www.notdiamond.ai/)[[10]](https://www.notdiamond.ai/blog/launching-not-diamond)
- **Cost-quality tradeoff**: Predicts per-query quality/cost; routes to optimize accuracy while cutting cost/latency (claims beat individual models). Custom training on production data for hyper-personalization/feedback loops.
- **Signals**: ML models (including random forests in RoRF) trained on prompt features/evaluation outcomes; supports embeddings or other representations for prediction of best model.
- **2025–2026 work**: Continued SDK/releases and custom training emphasis; positioned against open frameworks like RouteLLM.

### Martian
- **Overview and dates**: Commercial “first LLM router” (RouterBench arXiv 2403.12031 March 2024; active through 2025 with Accenture investment). Dynamically routes prompts to optimal LLM(s) across providers for quality at lowest cost; includes gateway features.[[11]](https://withmartian.com/post/introducing-routerbench)[[12]](https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2024/accenture-invests-in-martian-to-bring-dynamic-routing-of-large-language-queries-and-more-effective-ai-systems-to-clients)
- **Cost-quality tradeoff**: Model mapping/search finds best LLM per prompt; reported 20–97% cost cuts while matching/exceeding single-model performance. RouterBench evaluates routing systems comprehensively.
- **Signals**: Likely prompt embeddings/features + learned predictors (details less public); focuses on real-time quality prediction across many models.
- **2025–2026 work**: RouterBench as benchmark standard; enterprise integrations; comparisons in routing surveys.

### Multi-Signal Routers
vLLM Semantic Router is the clearest 2025–2026 exemplar, extracting/composing heterogeneous signals (classifiers + heuristics + embeddings + safety) via Boolean rules and plugin chains for flexible policies. Other production patterns combine domain classification, embeddings, keyword matching, latency/feedback scores, and benchmark priors.[[7]](https://vllm.ai/blog/2025-11-19-signal-decision)[[13]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/production-ready-llm-routing-patterns-pitfalls-jordan-leibowitz-oudqe) This outperforms single-signal (pure embedding or classifier) approaches in robustness and customizability.

### Cascade/Escalation Approaches
Common and theoretically grounded pattern: try cheap model first, then escalate on failure/uncertainty (schema validation, self-consistency, judge model, or confidence). Unified with routing in “cascade routing” frameworks (ICML 2025 paper). RL-based extensions (e.g., Router-R1 NeurIPS 2025) treat multi-round routing+aggregation as sequential decisions with cost-aware rewards.[[2]](https://openreview.net/forum?id=AAl89VNNy1)[[14]](https://www.truefoundry.com/blog/llm-routing-cost-quality-aware-model-selection)[[15]](https://tianpan.co/blog/2025-11-03-llm-routing-model-cascades) Often beats pure one-shot routing on cost when most traffic resolves cheaply; escalation rate is a key monitored variable.

**Overall ranking (prominence + advancement in mid-2026)**: vLLM Semantic Router (multi-signal production leader) > RouteLLM (strong open preference baseline) > Martian/NotDiamond (commercial maturity/benchmarks) > cascade/escalation (complementary, often hybridized) > pure multi-signal variants. Many systems now combine elements (e.g., semantic pre-filter + cascade).

### Gateway Evolution: From Intent/Namespace Routing to “Cheapest Model Meeting Measured Quality Bar”
Persist these signals/labels (store per request or aggregated):
- Query embeddings (or hashed equivalents) and/or classifier outputs (intent, complexity, difficulty, modality).
- Predicted quality score (from router or surrogate model).
- Actual quality labels/metrics (human prefs, LLM-as-judge scores, task-specific success like schema validity, user feedback, or downstream metrics) when available.
- Cost per query (or model pricing metadata).
- Model chosen + outcome (success/fail/escalation trigger, latency, tokens).
- Namespace/intent tags (for bootstrapping) + any safety/PII flags.
- Historical performance per (intent, model) pair for calibration.

**Simplest router design that beats static routing in 2026**: A lightweight (e.g., ModernBERT or small classifier/random-forest) *predicted-quality router* trained offline on your historical data. For each request:
1. Extract embedding + basic signals (intent/namespace).
2. Predict quality score per candidate model (or binary “meets bar”).
3. Select the cheapest model whose predicted quality ≥ your bar (threshold tuned on held-out data with actual quality labels).

This is a direct evolution of RouteLLM/NotDiamond-style predictors but thresholded on your quality metric instead of pairwise preference. Add a lightweight cascade fallback (escalate on low confidence or post-hoc check) for safety. Train periodically on persisted labels; start with your existing namespace data + synthetic augmentation. It beats static primary+fallback by dynamically avoiding over-provisioning on easy queries while guaranteeing the bar via prediction calibration. Multi-signal extensions (vLLM-style) or RL sequential variants can be layered later.[[16]](https://openreview.net/forum?id=8sSqNntaMr)[[17]](https://github.com/Not-Diamond/RoRF)

This design is deployable with existing gateways (add a small inference step before model call) and leverages the 2024–2026 open tooling.
